chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD Criminal Acquittal Appeal No.S-152 of 2024 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objections. 2. For orders on M.A. No.11402/2024(application u/s 5 of Limitation Act). 3. For orders on M.A. No.11403/2024(exemption application). 4. For hearing of M.A. No.342/2025 (application u/s 482 Cr.P.C). 5. For hearing of main case. 06.02.2025 Mr. Faisal Ali Raza Bhatti, Advocate for respondents / accused Mst. Noor Bano and Ali Hassan. Mr. Muhammad Saleem Laghari, Advocate for accused No.5. Mr. Shahid Ahmed Shaikh, Additional Advocate General, Sindh along-with SSP Aadil Memon (AIG Legal) on behalf of IG Sindh, S.P Mushtaque Ahmed ADIG, Legal C.P.O Karachi, SSP Tando Allahyar Abraiz Ahmed Abbasi, PDSP Ghulam Nabi Khoso, Legal Branch Hyderabad, PDSP, Aijaz Ali Ghori, Legal Branch Tando Allahyar, DSP Qasimabad Bashir Ahmed, SIP/ SHO Qasimabad Shoukat Ali, DSP Wazir Ali on behalf of DIGP Hyderabad and DSP/SDPO Latifabad Gul Hassan Thebo. == On the last date of hearing certain directions were passed by this Court, in compliance whereof, learned APG has filed compliance report on behalf of IG Police whereby submissions with respect to the directions of this Court have been made. (On query, AIGP submits that I.G police was unwell and hence unable to attend the Court today however, he is available with compliance report to assist this Court.) AIGP present in Court submits that in the earlier round of trial, the accused were acquitted however, appellant challenged their acquittal, as such, upon directions of this Court in the instant appeal a team was constituted and the culprits were arrested and certain articles were also recovered.

Mr. Shahid Ahmed Shaikh, Additional Advocate General, Sindh along-with SSP Aadil Memon (AIG Legal) on behalf of IG Sindh, S.P Mushtaque Ahmed ADIG, Legal C.P.O Karachi, SSP Tando Allahyar Abraiz Ahmed Abbasi, PDSP Ghulam Nabi Khoso, Legal Branch Hyderabad, PDSP, Aijaz Ali Ghori, Legal Branch Tando Allahyar, DSP Qasimabad Bashir Ahmed, SIP/ SHO Qasimabad Shoukat Ali, DSP Wazir Ali on behalf of DIGP Hyderabad and DSP/SDPO Latifabad Gul Hassan Thebo. == On the last date of hearing certain directions were passed by this Court, in compliance whereof, learned APG has filed compliance report on behalf of IG Police whereby submissions with respect to the directions of this Court have been made. (On query, AIGP submits that I.G police was unwell and hence unable to attend the Court today however, he is available with compliance report to assist this Court.) AIGP present in Court submits that in the earlier round of trial, the accused were acquitted however, appellant challenged their acquittal, as such, upon directions of this Court in the instant appeal a team was constituted and the culprits were arrested and certain articles were also recovered. He also refers to annexure-B along-with his report to show that the articles recovered by the police were handed over to Mr. Abdul Wahid son of Haji Abdul Ghaffar (complainant) and his signature at the end of the document is also available. The complainant Mr. Abdul Wahid, who is present in person along-with his daughter and son- in-law admits his signature after looking into the document(s) and agrees that he has received such articles which is in addition to the articles already received by Mr. Abdul Wahid before the trial Court. AIGP further informs that one Javed Machi has been arrested in case crime No.40 of 2025 and another accused namely Sultan alias Nano is also required in said case for further recovery and undertakes that full-fledged efforts shall be taken for recovery as also for arresting the culprits, and in this regard, he will be submitting an application for physical remand of Sultan alias Nano before the concerned Court during course of the day. Complainant Mr. Abdul Wahid was also heard who seeks further recovery of their remaining approximate 50% of their stolen articles and expresses satisfaction on the compliance report. Learned counsel for respondents / accused Mst. Noor Bano and Ali Hassan insists that instant acquittal appeal may be proceeded so the matter may come to an end. The compliance report is found satisfactory and personal attendance of the officials is dispensed with. The appeal to proceed on the next date. To come-up after four weeks as per roster. JUDGE Muhammad Danish *
